summary: This week in AI, major developments include Apple's lawsuit against OpenAI, the launch of new AI models, and advancements in AI-powered tools and technologies. The industry is seeing significant investments and innovations, with a focus on improving AI safety, security, and accessibility.
insight: The increasing use of AI in various industries is leading to a rise in concerns about job displacement, data privacy, and security, highlighting the need for responsible AI development and deployment.
highlights: Apple files a trade secrets lawsuit against OpenAI, potentially disrupting its IPO plans.
New AI models, such as Kimi 3, are being launched, closing the gap with existing models like Anthropic's Opus 4.8.
Google is expanding its AI Mode, allowing users to link and interact with select apps, and renaming its AI note-taking app to Gemini Notebook.

articles.title: Kimi: Threat or menace?
articles.description: Chinese company Moonshot AI released a new version of its Kimi model this week, prompting concern about "full AI communism."

articles.title: Databricks hits $188B valuation, extending its run as AI’s favorite second act
articles.description: Databricks has remade its image into an AI company and has published research on the cost savings of open weight AI models for coding.

articles.description: Agility is opening a new training center for its Digit robots in Fremont, California.
